theme_strategy.primary_archetype: Neo-Brutalist Playful (Swiss High-Contrast meets Vibrant Play)
theme_strategy.vibe: playful, unapologetic, tactile, highly engineered but irrevent
theme_strategy.rationale: To stand out as a dev in 2026, we avoid standard 'tech dark mode' (navy/blue-50) and generic SaaS grids. Instead, we use Neo-Brutalism: hard shadows, raw typography, and high-contrast vibrant accents against a matte background. It screams 'I know the rules so well I can break them gracefully.'

headings.usage_rules: Use for all H1-H3. ALWAYS uppercase, ALWAYS tracking-tighter.

body.usage_rules: Use for all body text, tags, dates, and small labels. Ground the playful headings with serious dev typography.

scale.h1: text-6xl sm:text-7xl md:text-8xl lg:text-[8vw] leading-none tracking-tighter uppercase font-black
scale.h2: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl leading-tight tracking-tight uppercase font-extrabold
scale.h3: text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl leading-snug tracking-tight font-extrabold

spacing_and_layout.container: w-full max-w-[1400px] mx-auto px-6 md:px-12 lg:px-24

motion.reveal: framer-motion: initial={opacity:0, y:50}, whileInView={opacity:1, y:0}, viewport={once:true, margin:'-100px'}

nav.layout: Floating pill at the BOTTOM center of the viewport (bottom-6). This is an unexpected, playful pattern.
nav.style: Glassmorphism: backdrop-blur-xl bg-surface/80 border-2 border-border rounded-full px-6 py-3 flex items-center gap-6 z-50.
nav.interactions: Links are monospace text. Hover: text turns primary color and a small dot appears below the link. The Resume button is a solid primary color pill inside the nav.

hero.layout: Grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 gap-8 items-center min-h-[90vh]. Heavy asymmetry.
hero.visuals: Name spans col-span-12, massive Cabinet Grotesk text wrapping naturally. The hero photo is in a raw rectangular div with a hard border and hard shadow (offset bottom-right). Overlap a smaller div containing the 'cat' image/icon.
hero.interactions: Make the cat element draggable using Framer Motion `<motion.div drag dragConstraints={{...}}>`. Cursor turns to 'grab'. Text staggers in on load.

experience.layout: Harsh vertical list, full width. No traditional vertical timeline line.
experience.visuals: Each role is a massive row with a solid bottom border (border-b-2). Left side: Dates in monospace. Middle: Huge uppercase Role Title. Right: Company Logo in a square hard-border box.
experience.interactions: Hovering a row fills its background with `accent_vibrant` (e.g., #CCFF00 in dark mode) and forces all text inside to black. The company logo rotates 5deg.

skills.layout: The 'Tetris' Bento Grid (cols-1 md:cols-8 lg:cols-12). Leave at least one cell entirely empty for breathing room.
skills.visuals: Each skill category is a card with a hard border and hard shadow. The category title is an overline label. The skills themselves are displayed as running marquee text inside the card, or a dense brutalist tag cloud.
skills.interactions: Hovering a card presses it down (reduces shadow, translates down/right to simulate physical button press).

projects.layout: Vertical stack of overlapping full-width rows, or massive cards.
projects.visuals: Left: Large screenshot with a harsh border. Right: Project title in Cabinet Grotesk, tech stack in mono tags. Use a loud, oversized arrow icon for the 'Live Link'.
projects.interactions: Hovering the project card reveals a diagonal marquee of the project name across the background.

education.layout: Two oversized 'concert tickets' or 'receipts' side by side.
education.visuals: Dashed borders (`border-dashed`), barcode SVG graphics at the top or bottom, raw monospace data layout (Key: Value).
education.interactions: Slight float animation up and down (yoyo).

theme_toggle.visuals: Chunky, physical-looking square button with hard drop shadow. Icon swaps between a brutalist Sun and Moon.
theme_toggle.interactions: Clicking presses the button physically (shadow reduces to 0). Optional: the page background color transition uses a circular reveal mask (if feasible), otherwise standard crossfade.

signature_moments: The Floating Bottom Nav: Moves the traditional header to an app-like dock at the bottom.
Draggable Cat: The user specifically mentioned a cat. Making it a physics-based draggable object is intensely playful.
Neo-Brutalist Hard Shadows: Replaces soft SaaS shadows with stark 100% opacity offset shadows, making the UI feel like physical stamped metal or cardboard.
Receipt Education Cards: Recontextualizes boring education data into tactile, physical objects.
implementation_instructions: DO NOT use Inter, Roboto, or standard sans-serifs. Inject the provided Google Fonts/Fontshare links in index.html or index.css.
Configure tailwind.config.js to extend colors with CSS variables (e.g., var(--bg-main)) so theme toggling is seamless.
Add 'dark' class to the HTML tag for dark mode handling.
Ensure Framer Motion's AnimatePresence is used if any elements are mounted/unmounted, though most are static.
For shadows, write custom utilities in tailwind.config.js like: `boxShadow: { 'hard': '6px 6px 0px 0px var(--border-color)', 'hard-hover': '2px 2px 0px 0px var(--border-color)' }`.
No shadcn or Next.js. Build raw HTML + Tailwind elements wrapped in Framer Motion motion.divs.
